title: V7 Analysis: Discarding the Bottleneck, Bridging to SAM's Native Text Space, and the Exponential LM Cliff

what_we_tried.bridge_to_sam_text_space: V1-V6 all used a square-bottleneck projector ending at 256-D (SAM's decoder query space), forcing Qwen's 4096-D [SEG] representations through an increasingly aggressive compression ratio. V4's 10.5M-param projector memorised ADE20K directions (Directional Drift). V4-Slim's 2.2M bottleneck (4096->512->256) fixed the drift but capped RWTD at ~0.73. V6 inherited that cap. V7 abandons the bottleneck entirely: Linear(4096, 1024) + LayerNorm + GELU + Dropout(0.4) projects into SAM3's NATIVE text-encoder width, then SAM3's own pretrained resizer (Linear 1024->256, kept FROZEN) maps to decoder space. We train the Bridge (4.2M params); the resizer brings SAM3's pretrained 1024->256 semantic mapping for free.
what_we_tried.exponential_lm_cliff: V6 cosine-decayed LM weights within each texture block (lambda_min=0.05 at [SEG], lambda_max=1.0 at block start) effectively kept LM pressure ~0.5 everywhere in the middle, producing a gentle tug-of-war between DICE (which wants raw geometric gradient on [SEG]) and LM (which wants linguistic fidelity on EVERY text token). Empirically V6 DICE plateaued at 0.43. V7 replaces the cosine with an exponential cliff: Weight(d) = 1 - exp(-2*d) where d = token-wise distance to the nearest ASSISTANT-region [SEG]. Weight at d=0 (SEG itself) is strictly 0.0; at d=1 it is 0.865; at d=2 it is 0.982. This isolates [SEG] geometrically while applying near-full LM pressure everywhere else, eliminating the tug-of-war.
what_we_tried.permanent_sam_freeze: V5 unfroze SAM cross-attn LoRA at Stage 3 and produced language collapse. V6b's double-decay curriculum stabilised language but still saw a -0.046 RWTD regression in Stage 3. A parallel ZS SAM 3 experiment (no VLM, object-aware prompt hand-fed) achieved 0.928 RWTD mIoU -- proving SAM natively handles our target distribution. Conclusion: the SAM LoRA was not helping; it was an OOD-regression mechanism. V7 freezes SAM permanently and drops the orthogonal LoRA entirely.
what_we_tried.universal_object_aware_prompt: V6's training prompt said 'NOT individual objects within it. Think of each region as a surface/area, not as a single object.' This made sense for pure-texture ADE20K training but worked against RWTD's object-rich scenes. V7's prompt adds an object-aware clause to the example ('a prominent foreground object and its background, or contrasting materials') while keeping the same IMPORTANT: surface/area directive. The format template also explicitly shows '<|seg|>' per TEXTURE line, giving Qwen instruction-output alignment. This last change breaks implicit assumptions: the literal '<|seg|>' in the prompt tokenises as the special SEG token, so four downstream extraction sites (create_labels, create_independent_texture_mask, extract_seg_hidden_states, inference_forward regex fallback) require filtering by the assistant-region boundary to avoid treating prompt-template SEGs as real model outputs.
what_we_tried.two_stage_curriculum: V5 and V6 used three stages. V6b's Stage 3 was the regression locus. V7 collapses the curriculum to two: Stage 1 (12 epochs) Bridge warmup with Qwen frozen at base LR 1e-4; Stage 2 (8 epochs) Qwen LoRA unfrozen at 1e-6 with Bridge decayed 10x to 1e-5. The extended Stage 1 (12 vs V6b's 10) reflects the larger Bridge's slower convergence.

the_zs_sam_breakthrough.finding: ZS SAM 3 with a highly descriptive, object-aware text prompt achieved 0.928 mIoU on RWTD
the_zs_sam_breakthrough.mechanism: The prompt was hand-crafted to treat objects-with-textures as natural regions, e.g. 'a conch shell shell with brown-and-white mosaic pattern' rather than 'smooth shell surface'. SAM's native text encoder (VETextEncoder with width=1024, output resizer 1024->256) fully understood and localised these descriptions without any fine-tuning.
the_zs_sam_breakthrough.implication: The geometric ceiling is 0.928, not V5-Oracle's 0.81. V1-V6 were all constrained by the projector, not by SAM. The correct move is to reduce the projector's surgery on the semantic signal, not train SAM harder.

the_v6_final_postmortem.finding: V6-Extended training completed 20 epochs, monotonic on val (0.62 -> 0.68) but regressed on RWTD (0.738 at ep10 -> 0.692 at ep15 live-inference-1-to-6)
the_v6_final_postmortem.mechanism: Stage 3 SAM LoRA adaptation concentrated representations onto the training-prompt distribution. Under structured-prompt ablations, ep18 dropped 0.111 mIoU below ep10. Classic domain memorisation.
the_v6_final_postmortem.implication: Val mIoU on ADE20K_DeTexture is a misleading proxy once Stage 3 fires. Honest OOD metric (exactly-2 live on RWTD) peaks at Stage 1 end.

the_bottleneck_smoking_gun.finding: V6 best-honest live RWTD mIoU = 0.694 (ep7, training prompt exactly-2). ZS SAM with object-aware prompt = 0.928. Delta = 0.234.
the_bottleneck_smoking_gun.mechanism: The only component between Qwen's rich 4096-D [SEG] hidden state and SAM's decoder is the projector. V1-V6 projectors all ended at 256-D (square bottleneck, 16x compression from 4096). Qwen's semantic richness -- open-vocabulary, multi-word descriptions -- gets smashed into 256 dimensions, losing the very richness that made ZS SAM work.
the_bottleneck_smoking_gun.solution: Widen the projection to 1024-D (matching SAM's native text-encoder width), use SAM's OWN pretrained 1024->256 projection for the final step. This lets Qwen produce semantically rich 1024-D vectors that flow through a pretrained channel SAM knows how to read.

what_this_predicts_but_has_not_yet_prove.h1_bridge_closes_the_zs_gap: If the bottleneck was the block, then V7 Bridge should push RWTD live exactly-2 mIoU from V6's 0.694 toward the ZS ceiling of 0.928. Success criterion: >= 0.80 by end of Stage 2. Strong success: >= 0.85.
what_this_predicts_but_has_not_yet_prove.h2_dice_finally_drops: V6's DICE loss plateaued at 0.43 because LM pressure was fighting it everywhere. With the exponential cliff, DICE should drop into the 0.2-0.3 range during Stage 1. Observable within the first 5 epochs.
what_this_predicts_but_has_not_yet_prove.h3_no_more_stage3_regression: With SAM permanently frozen, the OOD regression mechanism is gone. RWTD live mIoU should be monotonic across all 20 epochs (subject to Stage 2 transition noise at epoch 13).
what_this_predicts_but_has_not_yet_prove.h4_dropout_prevents_drift: The Bridge is 4.20M params (close to V4's Directional-Drift 10.5M in order of magnitude). Dropout(0.4) is the only regulariser. If H4 fails, we would see RWTD dropping 2+ epochs before plateau. Canary: RWTD live at ep8 should be >= RWTD live at ep4.

hypothesis_bridge_outperforms_bottleneck.claim: At the same Stage-1 checkpoint count, V7 Bridge has strictly higher RWTD honest mIoU than V6 bottleneck
hypothesis_bridge_outperforms_bottleneck.test: Compare V7 ep12 (Stage 1 end) live-exactly-2 to V6b ep10 live-exactly-2 (0.676) and V6-final ep10 live-standard (0.738 inflated).
hypothesis_bridge_outperforms_bottleneck.predicted_outcome: V7 ep12 live-exactly-2 > 0.75; if true, Bridge hypothesis CONFIRMED.

hypothesis_exponential_unlocks_dice.claim: The exponential cliff lets mask_dice drop where V6's cosine kept it at 0.43
hypothesis_exponential_unlocks_dice.test: Training log: monitor train_mask_dice every 100 steps. Compare trajectory to V6-Final run.
hypothesis_exponential_unlocks_dice.predicted_outcome: V7 Stage 1 end mask_dice < 0.35 (vs V6 Stage 1 end ~0.47).

hypothesis_frozen_sam_no_regression.claim: Permanent SAM freeze eliminates v6_completed's 0.713 -> 0.678 and V6-Final's 0.738 -> 0.692 Stage 3 RWTD regressions
hypothesis_frozen_sam_no_regression.test: RWTD live-standard mIoU at ep16, ep20. No negative delta between them.

v3.failure: LLM co-training: count collapse, projector drift, SAM regression

v4.failure: Directional Drift (10.5M projector memorises ADE20K directions)

v4_slim.breakthrough: Information Bottleneck fixes drift. First to beat ZS.

v5_oracle.breakthrough: Block-diagonal mask + bottleneck + SAM LoRA. Geometric ceiling on the bottleneck architecture.

v5_live.failure: LoRA language collapse from zero-weight text loss

v6_extended.breakthrough: Double-decay stabilises val; live inference > Oracle (Hungarian inflation + Qwen health)
v6_extended.failure: Projector still bottlenecking 234 mIoU points below ZS ceiling

zs_sam3_object_aware_prompt.evidence: SAM natively understands object-aware descriptions; fine-tuning hurts OOD

v7_finalised.status: Training from scratch. Bridge (4096->1024) + frozen SAM resizer (1024->256) + exponential LM + SAM permanently frozen. Predicted ceiling: 0.85+.

critical_insight: V1 through V6 all asked the wrong question: 'how do we train a better projector to get Qwen's semantics into SAM?' The ZS SAM 0.928 result on RWTD reframes the question: SAM already understands object-aware semantic descriptions. The projector's job is not to LEARN a mapping but to NOT DESTROY the one SAM already knows. The V7 Bridge + frozen resizer design is the minimal intervention: widen Qwen's output to match SAM's native text-encoder width, then hand the last 1024->256 projection back to SAM's own pretrained weights. Everything that used to be 'our learnable projector' is now just shape-matching; the semantic translation happens inside SAM's frozen infrastructure.
lessons_learned: Square bottlenecks compress Qwen's rich vocabulary before SAM can see it. Use the target model's native width instead and borrow its pretrained projection for the final step. Cross-model alignment is a shape problem, not a learning problem, when both endpoints are pretrained.

LM loss schedules that don't isolate the grounding token produce a constant tug-of-war between DICE and LM. An exponential cliff with weight(d=0)=0 is mathematically clean: the SEG is geometrically free, everything else is linguistically anchored.

The 'verify stability in-domain, then stress-test OOD' strategy from V4 paid off again. V6-Extended looked fine on val but regressed on RWTD. ADE20K_DeTexture + RWTD stays the right dual-evaluation regime.
Freezing is a first-class design choice. V5 and V6b all tried to cleverly unfreeze SAM; all backfired on OOD. 'Freeze forever' removes an entire class of failure modes.

Prompt format and training target must align. V6 trained with <|seg|> in labels but not in the user-visible prompt template. V7 makes this explicit. The downstream cost (prompt-side SEG filtering) is small and localised.

The true baseline is not 'zero-shot SAM with the generic texture prompt' (0.706) but 'zero-shot SAM with a well-crafted object-aware prompt' (0.928). Always verify the upper bound before deciding where to train.

proof_1_bridge_shapes_verified.claim: V7 Bridge + frozen resizer produces the expected (B, 7, 256) output from (B, 7, 4096) Qwen hidden states
proof_1_bridge_shapes_verified.experiment: Build V7 model, run random bf16 input through model._sam_resizer(model.bridge(x))
proof_1_bridge_shapes_verified.conclusion: Architecture wires correctly. Resizer is frozen in place.

proof_2_exponential_weights_correct.claim: Exponential LM weighting (alpha=2.0, SEG forced to 0) matches expected values
proof_2_exponential_weights_correct.experiment: Build a synthetic chat with 2 SEG tokens in the assistant region and 2 in the user prompt (V7 template side-effect). Run create_labels. Inspect per-position weights.
proof_2_exponential_weights_correct.conclusion: All weight values match closed-form 1 - exp(-2*d); prompt-template SEGs correctly filtered from weight construction.

proof_3_param_counts_match_blueprint.claim: V7 trainable parameter counts match the intended design
proof_3_param_counts_match_blueprint.experiment: Instantiate Qwen2SAMDeTexture(cfg) with V7 config and call num_trainable_params()
proof_3_param_counts_match_blueprint.conclusion: Bridge is 4.20M (dominant), total 8.23M trainable, matches expected +1.97M over V6.

paper_outline_implications.must_add_section: The Bridge-to-Native-Space section should replace the 'Information Bottleneck' section as the core projector contribution. The bottleneck is now a retired design choice, kept in the ablation table as a baseline.
paper_outline_implications.must_revise_figure_1: Projector block changes from '4096 -> 512 -> 256' to '4096 -> 1024 -> [frozen SAM resizer] -> 256'. Add the pale/dashed-border indicator for the frozen resizer.
paper_outline_implications.must_update_ablation_table: Add row: '+ Bridge to SAM native width (V7)'. Add row: 'Exponential LM cliff replaces cosine decay'. Remove the Stage-3 SAM-LoRA rows once V7 results are in.
paper_outline_implications.must_add_zs_breakthrough_narrative: The ZS SAM 3 object-aware prompt result (0.928) is the new motivating datapoint. It belongs in the introduction as the 'empirical ceiling' and reframes the entire projector discussion.
paper_outline_implications.honest_disclosure: Report ZS (0.928) as an upper bound; report training-prompt live-inference (Hungarian-inflated) AND exactly-K honest; explain the methodology clearly so the reader can judge the real gap.

next_steps.immediate: Launch V7 training from scratch. python -m training.train --config configs/detexture.yaml.
during_training_canaries: train_mask_dice drops below 0.35 by end of Stage 1 (H2 success)
Epoch 8 RWTD live mIoU >= Epoch 4 (H4 success: no drift)
No RWTD drop between ep16 and ep20 (H3 success)
post_training_ablations_planned: Exactly-2 vs standard 1-to-6 vs object-friendly live inference at ep12, ep16, ep20
Bridge Dropout sweep: 0.2, 0.4 (baseline), 0.6 -- does heavier dropout buy more OOD?
Exponential alpha sweep: 1.0, 2.0 (baseline), 4.0 -- sharper cliff helps or hurts?
Skip-Stage-2 ablation: train with Qwen frozen forever and see how close we get without language adaptation
next_steps.if_v7_reaches_0_85: Declare architecture converged. Write paper around this lineage.
next_steps.if_v7_plateaus_below_0_80: Hypothesis 1 partially failed -- projector wasn't the only bottleneck. Next candidates: LoRA rank, Qwen prompt reformulation, or revisit SAM's frozen resizer weights.
